other_observers: Mark Peterson, Brad Steger; First found, I believe, by Ken Pals
description: The bird, larger than a House Finch, smaller than an American Robin, was bright red, except for the black surrounding the red bill and encompassing the eyes. The legs were dark reddish, the eyes were dark. The head was topped by a tall, sharply-pointed crest. The tail was relatively long.Behaviors: Responded quickly to playback of Northern Cardinal song and calls by flying in to area in front of us and singing.Call: In response to playback, sang a bright, melodious, "whi-cheeer, whi-cheer, whi-cheer, whi-whi-whi-whi" a few times, with the number of introductory syllables and ending syllables varying during its response.Plumage: basic
